data.text/markdown:
Cloudflare Crawl API Analysis

This notebook explores how it appears the Cloudflare Crawl service will crawl a website. I used my own website as a target and ran several crawls in one day to see what data was collected, and what the web server logs looked like.

Crawl Results

Lets write some functions to help us analyze the Cloudflare Crawl data that we've downloaded for each snapshot. get_records() is an iterator for grabbing each record from the downloaded data.

data.text/markdown: summarize_records() will take the records iterator, and count the number of unique URLs that were included as well as the counts by status:
Now we can run the summarization for each snapshot:

data.text/markdown:
It seems a bit strange that each run seemed to yield different output?

Web Logs

We can also look at what the activity was like by observing the web logs. I partitioned the web log for each run into the snapshot directory. The web log can be turned into a DataFrame.

data.text/markdown: We can see how many IP addresses were involved in the crawling, and how many requests they performed:

data.text/html: <marimo-ui-element object-id='RGSE-0' random-id='b88c8410-a9c0-86a5-1327-c824aa99cc08'><marimo-table data-initial-value='[]' data-label='null' data-data='"[{\"ip\":\"104.28.165.71\",\"count\":11},{\"ip\":\"104.28.160.121\",\"count\":11},{\"ip\":\"104.28.165.229\",\"count\":11},{\"ip\":\"104.28.163.132\",\"count\":11},{\"ip\":\"104.28.153.98\",\"count\":11},{\"ip\":\"104.28.153.140\",\"count\":10},{\"ip\":\"104.28.165.14\",\"count\":10},{\"ip\":\"104.28.166.59\",\"count\":2},{\"ip\":\"104.28.158.139\",\"count\":2},{\"ip\":\"104.28.153.59\",\"count\":2}]"' data-total-rows='10' data-total-columns='1' data-max-columns='50' data-banner-text='""' data-pagination='true' data-page-size='10' data-field-types='[["count",["integer","int64"]]]' data-show-filters='true' data-show-download='true' data-show-column-summaries='false' data-show-data-types='true' data-show-page-size-selector='true' data-show-column-explorer='true' data-show-chart-builder='true' data-row-headers='[["ip",["string","str"]]]' data-has-stable-row-id='false' data-lazy='false' data-preload='false' data-download-file-name='"download"'></marimo-table></marimo-ui-element>

data.text/markdown: Now we can see how many unique URLs were requested for the crawl, and what amount of duplication there was:

Only 29% of the requests for this snapshot were unique! This is because the crawlers constantly request the same CSS and JavaScript needed for rendering each page. The requests for HTML only happened once.
We can look at how many requests happened over time by bucketing them.

data.text/html: <marimo-ui-element object-id='itlH-0' random-id='a007cf5e-be0e-b44a-d878-1b98d056157c'><marimo-table data-initial-value='[]' data-label='null' data-data='"[{\"ip\":\"104.28.153.88\",\"count\":405},{\"ip\":\"104.28.163.131\",\"count\":266},{\"ip\":\"104.28.161.242\",\"count\":232},{\"ip\":\"104.28.165.231\",\"count\":223},{\"ip\":\"104.28.153.132\",\"count\":212},{\"ip\":\"104.28.163.132\",\"count\":212},{\"ip\":\"104.28.163.81\",\"count\":201},{\"ip\":\"104.28.166.65\",\"count\":188},{\"ip\":\"104.28.166.121\",\"count\":186},{\"ip\":\"104.28.164.201\",\"count\":185}]"' data-total-rows='246' data-total-columns='1' data-max-columns='50' data-banner-text='""' data-pagination='true' data-page-size='10' data-field-types='[["count",["integer","int64"]]]' data-show-filters='true' data-show-download='true' data-show-column-summaries='true' data-show-data-types='true' data-show-page-size-selector='true' data-show-column-explorer='true' data-show-chart-builder='true' data-row-headers='[["ip",["string","str"]]]' data-has-stable-row-id='false' data-lazy='false' data-preload='false' data-download-file-name='"download"'></marimo-table></marimo-ui-element>

data.text/markdown:
Results Comparison

I ran data collection for https://inkdroid.org on multiple occasions, but they didn't all yield the same number of records.

data.text/markdown:
HTML

How does the HTML for different pages look?
